title: tolpis virgata subsp. grandiflora
intro: Tolpis virgata subsp. grandiflora, also known as grand-flowered Tolpis, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Tolpis virgata subsp. grandiflora include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a promising candidate for the development of new medicinal products. Tolpis virgata subsp. grandiflora can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
